tasks.description: Package initialization for the app directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose core package components. `from src.app.exceptions.custom import InvalidSignatureError, IdempotencyConflictError, TenantNotFoundError, PayloadValidationError`
Verify that `src.app` can be imported and that `InvalidSignatureError` is accessible via `src.app.exceptions.custom`.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the api directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose API components. `from src.app.api.v1.endpoints import router`
Verify that `src.app.api` can be imported and that `src.app.api.v1.endpoints.router` is accessible via `src.app.api.__init__` and `src.app.api.v1.__init__`.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the v1 api directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose V1 API components. `from src.app.api.v1.endpoints import router`

tasks.description: Test the v1 api package initialization.
tasks.spec_section: Verify that `src.app.api.v1` can be imported and that `src.app.api.v1.endpoints.router` is accessible.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the core directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose core components. `from src.app.core.config import Settings`, `from src.app.core.security import verify_hmac_signature`
Verify that `src.app.core` can be imported and that `Settings` and `verify_hmac_signature` are accessible.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the models directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose models. `from src.app.models.webhook import WebhookEvent, EventStatus`
Verify that `src.app.models` can be imported and that `WebhookEvent` and `EventStatus` are accessible.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the services directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose services. `from src.app.services.relay import process_webhook_event`
Verify that `src.app.services` can be imported and that `process_webhook_event` is accessible.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the exceptions directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose exceptions. `from src.app.exceptions.custom import InvalidSignatureError, IdempotencyConflictError, TenantNotFoundError, PayloadValidationError`
Verify that `src.app.exceptions` can be imported and that the custom exception classes are accessible.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the db directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose database components. `from src.app.db.session import get_db`
Verify that `src.app.db` can be imported and that `get_db` is accessible.

tasks.description: Package initialization for the cli directory.
tasks.spec_section: Expose CLI components. `from src.cli.admin import admin_cli`
Verify that `src.cli` can be imported and that `admin_cli` is accessible.

Implement a Settings class using `from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings`. It must include `DATABASE_URL: str`, `SECRET_KEY: str`, and `RETRY_LIMIT: int = 5`. Use absolute imports where necessary.
Test the `Settings` class to ensure `DATABASE_URL` and `SECRET_KEY` are correctly loaded from environment variables.

tasks.description: Custom exception classes for the application.
tasks.spec_section: Define the following exception classes: `InvalidSignatureError(Exception)`, `IdempotencyConflictError(Exception)`, `TenantNotFoundError(Exception)`, and `PayloadValidationError(Exception)`.
Test that `InvalidSignatureError`, `IdempotencyConflictError`, `TenantNotFoundError`, and `PayloadValidationError` can be raised and caught correctly.
Implement an asynchronous engine and session factory using `from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, AsyncSession, async_sessionmaker`. Provide a `get_db` dependency function.
Test the `get_db` generator function to ensure it yields an `AsyncSession` and closes it correctly.

Define `EventStatus` Enum with `RECEIVED`, `PROCESSING`, `DELIVERED`, `DEAD_LETTER`. Implement `WebhookEvent` model with `id: UUID`, `tenant_id: UUID`, `idempotency_key: str`, `payload: dict`, `status: EventStatus`, `retry_count: int`, and `next_retry_at: datetime` using `from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase` and `from sqlalchemy import Column, String, JSON, DateTime, Integer`.
Test the `WebhookEvent` model instantiation and ensure the `EventStatus` enum values are correct.

Implement `verify_hmac_signature(payload: bytes, signature: str, secret: str) -> bool`. Use `import hmac`, `import hashlib`, and `from src.app.exceptions.custom import InvalidSignatureError`. Use `hmac.compare_digest` for security.
Test the `verify_hmac_signature` function with valid signatures, invalid signatures, and missing signatures (expecting `InvalidSignatureError`).

tasks.description: Core logic for processing and relaying webhooks.
tasks.spec_section: Implement `process_webhook_event(tenant_id: str, payload: dict, signature: str, idempotency_key: str, secret: str) -> WebhookEvent`. It must use `from src.app.models.webhook import WebhookEvent, EventStatus`, `from src.app.core.security import verify_hmac_signature`, and `from src.app.exceptions.custom import ...`. It should handle signature verification and event creation.
Test the `process_webhook_event` function, specifically verifying that it correctly calls `verify_hmac_signature` and returns a `WebhookEvent` with `EventStatus.RECEIVED`.

Implement a FastAPI router with a `POST /webhooks` endpoint. It should accept `tenant_id`, `payload`, `X-Signature`, and `X-Idempotency-Key`. Use `from src.app.services.relay import process_webhook_event` and `from src.app.models.webhook import WebhookEvent`.
Initialize the FastAPI app using `from fastapi import FastAPI` and include the router from `src.app.api.v1.endpoints`.

tasks.description: Integration tests for the API and application.
tasks.spec_section: Test the `POST /v1/webhooks` endpoint using `TestClient`. Verify successful ingestion (201), invalid signature (403), and duplicate idempotency key (409). Also test the application startup.
Implement a CLI using `import click`. Provide a `replay` command that takes a `tenant_id` and updates the status of `DEAD_LETTER` events to `PROCESSING` in the database using `src.app.db.session`.
Test the `replay` command using `click.testing.CliRunner`. Verify that it correctly identifies and updates events with `EventStatus.DEAD_LETTER` to `PROCESSING`.
